_comment: Guidance-pair drift manifest (Task C2.1, ADR-0005/0016). Consumed by
scripts/check_guidance_drift.py. Each rule is a semantic anchor that must be
present on BOTH sides of a pair; 'pattern'/'patterns' apply to both sides,
'claude_pattern(s)'/'codex_pattern(s)' override per side when wording differs.
Patterns are case-insensitive regexes searched with DOTALL.
documented_differences records intentional or pending one-sided content:
the validator reports them as informational, never as failures. Silent
divergence (an anchor missing on either side) fails the check.
Paths are workspace-root-relative; a leading ~/ resolves against the user home.
This file is agent-neutral shared authority (same class as TEAM_PROTOCOL.md):
either agent may propose changes via coordination messages; adding a rule or a
documented difference requires the pair files to actually justify it.

rules.description: 03_PKE_SA_NQA1 is the workspace root; Enconet/Ekonerg/TEKOL are project entries
Nested guidance may add rules but must not weaken evidence/validation requirements
ADR-0016 reciprocal infrastructure ban (each agent edits only its own side)
codex_patterns: Do not modify or re-index Claude-owned

rules.description: ADR-0017 coordination via immutable author-prefixed messages
ADR-0018 active queue vs archive with resolution manifests
Controlled documents and approved decisions outrank historical exports
docs/context/ is non-authoritative unless promoted
No in-place modification of controlled evidence or sieving/DATA
Repair/migration scripts require target-root, dry-run, backup review
Never report skipped/blocked/unrun validation as passed
Session start reads handoff, BOARD, unread messages, active claims
Session end uses the shared/user-global handoff skill (mandatory)
Workspace-wide engineering documentation lives in doc/
Verify actual tree/Git state before trusting copied paths or status
Planned files are not evidence of implemented capability

documented_differences.description: Skill install locations are tool-specific (.claude/skills vs .agents/skills; Codex additionally distinguishes user-global $HOME/.agents/skills).
Each side names only its own tool indexes (Claude: PKE_SA_NQA1_Enconet_docs/_controlled, Enconet-0a063bd7; Codex: Codex_global_guidance, PKE_SA_NQA1_codex_guidance, PKE_SA_NQA1_agent_guidance).
scripts/agent_coord.py is named in Claude-side protocol only; Codex adoption requested in CC_2026-07-11T211205Z_c2-4-complete-coordination-infrastructure (pending).

Claude side records pytest/pandas/openpyxl as installed (C5.3, 2026-07-11); Codex side still says the environment may lack them. Codex-side refresh requested in CC_2026-07-11T212041Z_c5-3-complete-deps-and-tests (pending).
Claude side points repository facts to doc/AS-IS.md; Codex side predates C0.1/C0.2 and still carries the unverified-Git constraint (pending Codex-side refresh).

rules.description: Read-first names MASTER_DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md as canonical
Read-first names docs/ALIGNMENT_PLAN.md as canonical
Read-first names decisions/README.md (ADR register)
Read-first names docs/CX_CC_RECONCILIATION.md
docs/context/ and docs/_archive/ are historical input, not a contract
Working code under sieving/src/json_extractor
Tests under sieving/tests
Do not run fix_files.py / fix_structure.py (unsafe root assumptions)
No export after a filter error without recorded explicit approval
Resolve active paths from roots/config, never legacy paths
Mandatory verification: pytest from Enconet/sieving plus verify_install.py
Use the user-global handoff skill to close/pause/transfer sessions

documented_differences.description: Streamlit GUI retirement (owner decision 2026-07-04) is recorded on the Claude side only; Codex-side mirror pending a Codex-authored update.
Appendix B taxonomy/schema synchronization guardrail is Codex-side only; Claude-side mirror pending.
Codex read-first additionally lists Sieving_method_specification_Guide.md with the S10.1 caveat (C4.4); Claude side references the defect indirectly via workspace rules.
Explicit frozen-plan wording (master plan / alignment plan not editable without owner reopening) is Codex-side only; Claude side relies on the authority section.

rules.description: Never convert missing information or an unrun check into implied success
Identical minimum frontmatter fields
Overall status is complete|partial|blocked with the same semantics
Nine body headings, identical names and order
Timestamped records are never overwritten
Absent Git handled via git_state: unavailable and nogit suffix; no git init during handoff
HANDOFF.md pointer is refreshed only after the immutable record validates
rules.pattern: Do not publish the pointer first

rules.description: Never include secrets, raw controlled evidence, or credentials
Absent log recorded as log_update: not-configured, never invented

documented_differences.description: source_agent is claude-code on the Claude side and codex on the Codex side (by design).

Four-valued per-check states (passed|failed|not-run|unknown) and the project-specific notes section are currently Claude-side only; contract alignment per ADR-0014 says changes must update both sides.
Each side reads its own guidance chain first (CLAUDE.md vs AGENTS.md); the Claude side adds an explicit project-neutrality preamble.
